# Game Translation

This file exists to answer the question: "How does ancient China become game?"

## Core Rule

Do not stop at historical explanation.

When this skill is triggered for Zongzu, aim to produce at least one of:
- a mechanic
- a read model
- a map or node abstraction
- a desk-sandbox interaction
- a conflict or campaign presentation pattern
- a naming or descriptor set

When the user asks to deepen something, aim to produce more than one layer:
- institution
- class or actor ladder
- public surface
- pressure loop
- failure mode

## First-Class Topic Conversion Contract

Any major topic in this skill should be convertible as part of one living-world system.

Do not leave major topics as isolated leaves if the user is asking at system scale.

A good first-class conversion usually includes:
- the topic itself
- one adjacent institution or power layer
- one public surface
- one consequence or escalation path
- one module landing

## What the Repo Wants

From the repo docs, the target fantasy is not:
- a raw spreadsheet
- a pure text parser
- a card battler
- a tactical battlefield game
- a free-roam open world

It is closer to:
- clan-head simulation
- multi-route social simulation
- spatialized family governance
- desk sandbox
- local pressure board
- rebellion and polity-forming arc
- warfare-lite campaign board later

## Full-Skill Conversion

When the user wants the whole skill connected, convert the world in this order:
- era and region frame
- settlement and route ecology
- household and lineage reproduction
- material production, storage, debt, and transport
- institutional mediation through halls, schools, temples, yamen, and brokers
- coercion through retainers, clerks, office conflict, disorder, and campaign pressure
- public visibility through rumor, notice, fairs, funerals, and crowd reaction
- death, succession, and legitimacy aftershocks
- module and surface ownership

The point is not to make every answer longer.

The point is to make broad answers come out linked.

So when converting history to gameplay, ask:
- what arrives at the hall
- what appears on the lineage surface
- what becomes a node or route on the desk sandbox
- what belongs to lineage power and what belongs to ordinary-household survival
- what route variety exists beyond inheritance and office
- what pushes disorder from raid to movement to regime
- what becomes a conflict vignette
- what becomes a later campaign-board pressure layer

## Conflict-Scale Conversion

When the user asks for battle, combat, or warfare, decide first:
- should this stay as pressure on the desk sandbox
- should this become a local conflict vignette
- should this become a tactical-lite encounter
- should this escalate into a campaign board

Default preference for this repo:
- pressure first
- vignette second
- tactical-lite only when needed
- campaign board when the conflict spans routes, depots, crossings, or multiple settlements

## Sandbox-Structure Conversion

When the user asks for sandbox structure, convert the world into:
- structural layers
- node grammar
- route grammar
- overlay families
- desk read order
- intervention verbs

The point is not bigger maps.

The point is clearer pressure reading.

## Wargame-Flow Conversion

When the user asks for `bingqi` or warfare flow, convert the design into:
- scale choice
- situation read
- command intent
- force commitment
- route commitment
- contact or non-contact result
- clash resolution
- aftermath return

## Force-Family Conversion

When the user asks about armed categories, convert them by:
- authorization source
- support source
- mobility
- hold capacity
- public legitimacy
- likely fallout

## Era and Regional Framing

Read [late-imperial-pack.md](late-imperial-pack.md) when you need a strong default frame, [jiangnan-water-network-county.md](jiangnan-water-network-county.md) when you need a water-network county, [north-china-road-county.md](north-china-road-county.md) when you need a dry-road county, [southwest-mountain-borderland.md](southwest-mountain-borderland.md) when you need a mountain borderland, [border-garrison-fort-belt.md](border-garrison-fort-belt.md) when you need a militarized border zone, and [simulation-calibration.md](simulation-calibration.md) when the design needs believable bands.

Ask:
- what exact period is required and what can remain labeled baseline
- what landform or route ecology makes this place feel Chinese in a specific way
- which regional pack should carry the scene before inventing custom jargon
- what should be slow, pulsed, capacity-bound, or season-bound

Regional contrast is one of the fastest ways to escape generic ancient-fantasy flattening.

The same mechanic should feel different across:
- a Jiangnan water-network county
- a north-China road county
- a southwest mountain borderland
- a border garrison and fort belt

## Local-Culture Conversion

Read [local-culture-customs-regional-identity.md](local-culture-customs-regional-identity.md), [festival-folkways-entertainment.md](festival-folkways-entertainment.md), and [public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md](public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md) when a place has geography but still lacks social voice.

Ask:
- what feels locally honorable, shameful, boorish, refined, or suspicious in public
- what foodways, dress cuts, phrases, songs, notices, and ritual habits make one county read differently from another
- how dialect distance and outsider friction change mediation, recruitment, marriage, schooling, and market dealing
- which institutions feel locally rooted rather than merely imposed from above

## Influence and Power Conversion

Read [influence-power-factional-pull.md](influence-power-factional-pull.md), [gentry-local-magnates.md](gentry-local-magnates.md), [official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md](official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md), and [public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md](public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md) when the design needs more than one vague "influence" stat.

Ask:
- what kind of power is actually in play here
- who holds it and who only appears to hold it
- what it can realistically move: marriage, petition, law, debt, route safety, punishment, protection, or succession
- what it costs to use and what backlash it creates
- what part is visible in hall, notice, rumor, office, or conflict surfaces

Good outputs include:
- influence families
- faction pull maps
- who-blocks-whom matrices
- leverage ladders
- conversion limits between prestige, debt, office, coercion, and legitimacy

## Rebellion and Dynasty-Cycle Conversion

Read [rebellion-dynasty-cycle.md](rebellion-dynasty-cycle.md) when the design needs to move from county pressure into macro-political rupture.

Do not model the long arc as:
- random rebels appear
- one battle happens
- a kingdom is born

Prefer a ladder:
- local distress
- bandit concentration
- protection failure
- family militarization or side-taking
- rebel coalition
- provisional governance
- legitimacy claim
- dynastic consolidation
- dynastic fatigue
- regime fracture and collapse

Good outputs include:
- escalation tracks
- side-taking matrices
- regime-claim objects
- dynasty-name or era-name hooks
- collapse-pressure overlays on the map and campaign board

## Commoner and Dynamic-Society Conversion

Read [lineage-commoner-relations.md](lineage-commoner-relations.md), [commoner-livelihoods.md](commoner-livelihoods.md), [multi-route-play.md](multi-route-play.md), and [route-families.md](route-families.md) when the design risks becoming "family tree only."

Do not assume every meaningful life path is:
- inherit
- study
- become an official

Do not harden the result into fixed route trees.

Good social layers for this project can be grouped as:
- social governance
- lineage management
- commoner household management
- bandit management
- commercial management
- official career progression
- imperial governance

Under those layers, you can still place more specific subroutes such as steward, tenant, workshop, broker, boatman, clerk, escort, migrant, or shadow survival.

These layers and subroutes should touch:
- different nodes
- different pressures
- different social dependencies
- different narrative vocabularies
- and allow movement between positions over time

## Lineage Institution Conversion

Read [lineage-institutions-corporate-power.md](lineage-institutions-corporate-power.md) when the design needs `zongzu` as organized power instead of only kin feeling.

Ask:
- what makes this lineage an institution rather than a surname list
- what assets, rules, halls, schools, charities, and mediation rights it controls
- how that corporate power touches ordinary households and state actors

## Lineage Private-Force Conversion

Read [lineage-private-force-retainers-conflict.md](lineage-private-force-retainers-conflict.md), [local-order-militia-banditry.md](local-order-militia-banditry.md), and [gentry-local-magnates.md](gentry-local-magnates.md) when lineage coercion should exist below formal war.

Ask:
- what kind of private force exists here: hall guards, escorts, estate retainers, `jiading`, or feud mobilization
- whether the force is for deterrence, debt collection, prestige defense, escort work, or revenge
- how violence pressure becomes visible before full open conflict
- what makes this socially embedded rather than just another army list

## Imperial and Bureaucratic Conversion

Read [imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md](imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md) and [exams-offices-clerks.md](exams-offices-clerks.md) when the design needs emperor, magistrates, clerks, commoners, and townspeople to feel linked rather than isolated.

Ask:
- where decree becomes paperwork
- where paperwork becomes pressure
- where townspeople and commoners actually feel state reach
- which part is imperial theater and which part is clerical reality

## Education and Literacy Conversion

Read [education-literacy-schools-academies.md](education-literacy-schools-academies.md), [exams-offices-clerks.md](exams-offices-clerks.md), and [lineage-institutions-corporate-power.md](lineage-institutions-corporate-power.md) when the design needs more than a narrow exam ladder.

Ask:
- who can read a notice, draft a petition, keep accounts, or resist clerk manipulation
- what kinds of schools exist locally and who can plausibly enter them
- how book access, copying, tutoring, and academy prestige reshape faction, prestige, and ambition
- where practical literacy matters more than formal degree rank

## Living-World Texture Conversion

Read [disease-lifespan-death.md](disease-lifespan-death.md), [childhood-generations.md](childhood-generations.md), [body-desire-taboo.md](body-desire-taboo.md), and [information-messaging-documents.md](information-messaging-documents.md) when the world feels too mechanical or too bloodless.

Ask:
- who gets sick and who absorbs the care burden
- who is still a child, who is aging, and who is nearing replacement
- what desire, shame, vice, or scandal bends decisions
- how long news takes to arrive and how badly it mutates on the way

## Material-Life and Productive-Rhythm Conversion

Read [material-life-clothing-food-housing-travel.md](material-life-clothing-food-housing-travel.md), [agrarian-calendar-waterworks-hazards.md](agrarian-calendar-waterworks-hazards.md), [crafts-guilds-workshops.md](crafts-guilds-workshops.md), and [medicine-healing-burial.md](medicine-healing-burial.md) when the world needs stronger bodily, economic, and spatial texture.

Ask:
- what people wear, store, burn, eat, repair, or carry that materially changes household pressure
- what part of the year is slack, pinched, flooded, drought-hit, or repair-heavy
- where workshops, tools, orders, and apprentices create a different town rhythm from field labor
- how healing, funerals, and burial create cost, delay, memory, and visible public consequences

## Social Reproduction and Life-Course Conversion

Read [fertility-demography-infant-mortality.md](fertility-demography-infant-mortality.md), [women-life-cycle-gendered-experience.md](women-life-cycle-gendered-experience.md), and [marriage-gender-household-power.md](marriage-gender-household-power.md) when the design needs households to reproduce themselves unevenly rather than existing as static units.

Ask:
- who is being born, who survives, and who becomes a dependency wave rather than a future worker
- how childbirth, widowhood, remarriage, and elder female authority reshape household strategy
- where natal kin, affinal kin, and lineage expectation pull in different directions
- what part of continuity depends on women carrying work, risk, and reputation rather than on men issuing claims

## Property, Contract, and Credit Conversion

Read [property-contract-debt-credit.md](property-contract-debt-credit.md), [lineage-inheritance.md](lineage-inheritance.md), and [law-litigation-punishment.md](law-litigation-punishment.md) when ownership, debt, dowry, or contract pressure should produce leverage and conflict.

Ask:
- what asset type is actually in play and who can plausibly claim it
- whether the claim rests on custom, witness, seal, or brute power
- how debt or pledged property traps future choices instead of only creating a price
- where shame, repossession, petitioning, and broker intervention become publicly visible

## Law, Petition, and Mediation Conversion

Read [law-litigation-punishment.md](law-litigation-punishment.md), [official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md](official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md), and [imperial-local-bargaining-governance-friction.md](imperial-local-bargaining-governance-friction.md) when dispute resolution should become a living pressure system rather than a simple verdict table.

Ask:
- who can realistically file, draft, witness, sponsor, or block a petition
- whether the pressure runs through hall mediation, clerk delay, magistrate judgment, or face-saving settlement
- what part of the threat is punishment and what part is seizure, stigma, or procedural exhaustion
- how the outcome bends later marriage, debt, office access, route safety, or local standing

## Public Works and Transport-Artery Conversion

Read [public-works-granaries-canal-transport.md](public-works-granaries-canal-transport.md), [agrarian-calendar-waterworks-hazards.md](agrarian-calendar-waterworks-hazards.md), and [disaster-famine-relief-granaries.md](disaster-famine-relief-granaries.md) when routes, granaries, ferries, canals, or repair labor should shape the map as political infrastructure.

Ask:
- what route or storehouse is an artery rather than just a node
- who pays the maintenance burden and who captures the benefit
- when state-priority transport overrides local convenience
- how breakdown, dredging, breach, or queueing should alter trade, petitioning, and rebel opportunity

## Status-Bound Household Conversion

Read [status-groups-military-craft-households.md](status-groups-military-craft-households.md), [imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md](imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md), and [warfare-mobilization-supply-merit.md](warfare-mobilization-supply-merit.md) when inheritable duty and constrained mobility should matter.

Ask:
- which households are merely poor and which are actually bound by service identity
- how status lock changes labor freedom, mobility, pride, stigma, and evasion
- where state claim shows up as regular summons, expected repair, workshop duty, or military readiness
- what would it take for a household to escape, hide, or convert its obligation

## Death and Succession Shock Conversion

Read [death-succession-mourning-political-shock.md](death-succession-mourning-political-shock.md) and [medicine-healing-burial.md](medicine-healing-burial.md) when a death needs to keep working across household, lineage, office, army, or regime layers.

Ask:
- what work stops immediately and what burden gets reassigned
- what mourning suspends, distorts, or delays
- what inheritance, adoption, appointment, or command dispute opens after the loss
- which public surfaces turn the death into rumor, legitimacy, fear, or opportunism

## Imperial Sovereignty Conversion

Read [imperial-sovereignty-legitimacy-succession.md](imperial-sovereignty-legitimacy-succession.md), [imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md](imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md), and [court-eunuchs-outer-kin.md](court-eunuchs-outer-kin.md) when the design needs `huangquan` rather than only bureaucratic reach.

Ask:
- what in this problem belongs to throne legitimacy rather than local office mechanics
- whether succession clarity or reign change should alter backlog, rumor, obedience, and faction heat
- how symbolic center, ritual repair, mercy, and punishment tone reach county life without pretending symbols always work
- where emperor-centered confidence fails and local actors begin free-playing the field

## Imperial-Local Bargaining Conversion

Read [imperial-local-bargaining-governance-friction.md](imperial-local-bargaining-governance-friction.md), [imperial-sovereignty-legitimacy-succession.md](imperial-sovereignty-legitimacy-succession.md), and [gentry-local-magnates.md](gentry-local-magnates.md) when top-down power should arrive through negotiation rather than teleportation.

Ask:
- where an imperial order is softened, delayed, staged, or captured on its way downward
- which local actors buffer the center and which actors exploit the center's language
- how paper obedience differs from lived compliance
- whether households can appeal upward against local extraction without ever escaping the chain

## Official Private-Interest Conversion

Read [official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md](official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md), [exams-offices-clerks.md](exams-offices-clerks.md), and [property-contract-debt-credit.md](property-contract-debt-credit.md) when office life should feel braided with household strategy and private motive.

Ask:
- what family costs, schooling burdens, marriage obligations, or debts are bending this official's choices
- where clerks, gifts, or kin ties mediate access and decision speed
- what part of the behavior reads as necessity, greed, fear, reputation management, or lineage strategy
- how private pull leaves public traces in backlog, leniency, extraction, favoritism, or silence

## Office Conflict Conversion

Read [office-conflict-faction-yamen-struggle.md](office-conflict-faction-yamen-struggle.md), [imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md](imperial-power-bureaucracy-and-subjects.md), and [official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md](official-private-interest-family-entanglement.md) when bureaucracy should fight with itself.

Ask:
- where conflict is happening: memorial channel, appointment ladder, yamen desk, clerk room, or public notice layer
- what kind of weapon is being used: accusation, delay, docket capture, split clerk handling, rumor, or blocked recommendation
- how office conflict changes what ordinary households actually feel from the state
- whether the conflict freezes administration, redirects extraction, or poisons legitimacy

## Religion and Governance Conversion

Read [religion-temples-ritual-brokerage.md](religion-temples-ritual-brokerage.md) and [religion-state-and-popular-practice.md](religion-state-and-popular-practice.md) when religion needs to shape legitimacy, fear, charity, compliance, or public gathering.

Ask:
- what is official, what is local, and what is household ritual
- where religion acts as governance support versus governance anxiety
- how omens, temple prestige, and heterodoxy fear alter public mood

## Seasonal and Public-Life Conversion

Read [festival-folkways-entertainment.md](festival-folkways-entertainment.md) and [public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md](public-opinion-reputation-public-spaces.md) when the world needs to feel socially visible rather than privately simulated.

Ask:
- where people gather when they are not at home, at work, or at war
- what recurring calendar moments compress trade, ritual, gossip, matchmaking, theft, or petitioning
- where a notice, scandal, or proclamation becomes publicly real
- which spaces produce crowd courage, panic, mockery, or prestige

## Translation Ladder

Use this ladder:

1. Historical pattern
   Example: local order depends on formal yamen power, lineage shielding, militia or escort activation, and rumor.

2. Simulation variable
   Example: `local_order_pressure`, `response_activation_state`, `escort_strain`, `clan_shielding`.

3. Player-facing affordance
   Example: "send retainers", "delay retaliation", "petition county office", "quietly compensate", "mobilize escort".

4. Spatial surface
   Example:
   - hall visitor report
   - flashing hotspot on desk sandbox
   - route marker changing from stable to unsafe
   - conflict vignette showing wounded return or funeral

5. Narrative explanation
   Example: "The granary route is still open, but escort strain has worsened and village confidence is failing."

## Deep-Dive Stack

When the user asks for a deeper pass, stack:
- one topic file
- one adjacent topic from [topic-combination-matrix.md](topic-combination-matrix.md)
- one public-life or state-reach file
- one regional or calibration file if the answer risks flattening

## Ancient Sandbox Conversion

Read [ancient-sandbox.md](ancient-sandbox.md) for the detailed desk-sandbox pattern and [china-map-prefecture-county-routes.md](china-map-prefecture-county-routes.md) for Chinese ancient hierarchy and route topology.

Ancient sandbox does not mean one giant historical atlas.

Prefer medium abstraction:
- estates
- roads
- ferries
- waterworks
- markets
- academies
- yamen or office nodes
- shrines or ancestral surfaces
- security hotspots
- fronts and supply routes later

Good questions:
- what nodes matter socially
- what routes matter economically
- what places matter ritually
- what places matter for local coercion

Avoid:
- decorative map clutter with no systems meaning
- province-level grand strategy when the actual loop is local lineage pressure
- tactical hexes unless the product explicitly changes direction

## Wargame and Campaign-Board Conversion

Read [campaign-board.md](campaign-board.md) for the detailed warfare-lite board pattern.
Read [military-thought-and-command-culture.md](military-thought-and-command-culture.md) when the user explicitly wants `bingfa`, command culture, or a more Chinese-ancient warfare feel.

This project explicitly rejects detached tactical battle as the core form.

So when users ask for `bingqi`, interpret that through the repo's lens:
- command board
- route pressure board
- front posture board
- mobilization and supply board
- aftermath board

Translate historical warfare into:
- authority gates
- mobilization pools
- command cap
- supply state
- morale state
- route safety
- regional pressure
- campaign aftermath
- intelligence confidence
- deception windows
- timing advantage

Player-facing levers should be high-level:
- commit or hold reserves
- push route repair
- accelerate muster
- protect grain line
- force pursuit
- accept consolidation

Avoid:
- per-unit micro
- click-to-flank battlefield tactics
- making battle maps the dominant form

## Map Abstraction Patterns

For Chinese ancient administrative and route topology, also read [china-map-prefecture-county-routes.md](china-map-prefecture-county-routes.md).

### Local Desk Sandbox

Best for MVP and lite systems.

Represent:
- households or branch clusters
- estate zones
- roads and river links
- market nodes
- academy nodes
- office or yamen node
- security hotspots

Useful overlays:
- order
- grudge concentration
- market disruption
- migration pressure
- exam opportunity access

### Campaign Board

Best for post-MVP warfare extension.

Represent:
- fronts
- routes
- depots
- crossings
- regional support
- supply posture
- morale posture
- directive posture

Useful markers:
- command fit
- route risk
- supply break risk
- pursuit opportunity
- exhausted militia or escort drag

## UI Language Guidance

When inventing labels for map and board surfaces:
- prefer concise, weighty, administrative or lineage-facing wording
- avoid modern military dashboard jargon
- avoid fantasy-RPG battle wording

Good tone:
- memorial, dispatch, ledger, yamen, lineage, route, grain, levy, escort, pressure, aftermath

## Deliverable Templates

When asked to help with game design, try to answer in this shape:

### Mechanic Sketch
- historical basis
- game abstraction
- owning module
- inputs
- outputs
- visible surface
- intentional simplification

### Map Sketch
- node types
- route types
- overlays
- interaction verbs
- explanation text

### Route Sketch
- route fantasy
- access conditions
- main pressures
- key nodes and routes touched
- failure states
- crossover with lineage or office systems

### Dynamic Society Matrix
- social layer
- typical starting positions
- mobility triggers
- core verbs
- main resources
- main risks
- crossover pressures

### Institutional Power Sketch
- institution type
- corporate assets
- authority channels
- ordinary-household touchpoints
- public legitimacy
- failure or capture modes

### Lineage Armed-Conflict Sketch
- force type
- owning lineage or estate layer
- trigger patterns
- intimidation or violence surfaces
- public fallout
- escalation and stop conditions

### Conflict Vignette Sketch
- conflict scale
- incident location
- actors and force families
- trigger and immediate objective
- route or node stress
- civilian exposure
- aftermath outputs

### Sandbox Structure Sketch
- spatial slice
- structural layers
- node families
- route families
- overlay families
- desk read order
- intervention verbs

### Wargame Flow Sketch
- scale choice
- situation read
- command intent
- force commitment
- route commitment
- contact result
- clash result
- aftermath return

### Force Family Sketch
- force family
- authorization source
- support source
- mobility and hold profile
- best use cases
- legitimacy and fallout

### Deep-Dive Expansion
- core topic
- adjacent topics pulled in
- institution layer
- actor ladder
- public surfaces
- material flows
- failure modes
- era or region cautions

### Full-Skill World Sketch
- era baseline
- region pack and route ecology
- settlement and node grammar
- household and lineage reproduction chain
- material and debt loop
- institutional mediation layer
- coercion and conflict channels
- public-surface signals
- death and legitimacy aftermath
- owning modules and visible shell surfaces

### Law and Petition Sketch
- dispute type
- hall versus yamen jurisdiction
- petition access and friction
- mediation brokers or blockers
- visible punishment or stigma channel
- downstream effect on debt, marriage, office, or reputation

### State-Society Crosswalk
- imperial layer
- magistrate and yamen layer
- clerk and runner layer
- lineage and broker layer
- commoner and townsfolk touchpoints
- public surfaces where policy becomes lived pressure

### Era and Region Frame
- period baseline
- region pack
- route ecology
- local culture layer
- public-space texture
- scale assumptions
- explicit uncertainties
- contrast notes versus another region

### Living World Matrix
- life stage pressures
- illness and mortality effects
- desire and taboo pressures
- rumor and document channels
- emotional or crowd triggers

### Material Life Sketch
- subsistence profile
- clothing and shelter baseline
- travel and carrying constraints
- seasonal labor rhythm
- workshop or repair ecology
- healing and burial obligations
- visible public signals

### Local Culture Sketch
- local custom anchors
- dialect and phrasing cues
- outsider versus insider behavior
- customary legitimacy points
- schooling and temple style
- public pride or stigma surfaces

### Power Topology Sketch
- power families in play
- primary holders
- reach and limits
- conversion paths
- visible public surfaces
- backlash and decay paths
- likely blockers and brokers

### Education and Literacy Sketch
- literacy baseline
- school and academy access
- book and copying ecology
- practical document competence
- study burden versus labor need
- status and mobility effects

### Social Reproduction Sketch
- fertility and child-survival profile
- life-cycle stages in play
- widowhood and remarriage pressure
- natal versus affinal pull
- heir and continuity anxiety
- gendered labor and authority shifts

### Contract and Debt Sketch
- asset types in dispute
- claim basis
- debt pressure and collateral
- public or legal pressure points
- likely default or repossession path
- memory and shame residue

### Public Works Sketch
- artery or infrastructure object
- upkeep burden
- civilian versus state priority
- queue, delay, or breach behavior
- legitimacy and resentment effects
- visible map and board signals

### Status Obligation Sketch
- status group
- inherited duty
- mobility constraints
- extraction pattern
- evasion or release paths
- visible identity markers

### Death Shock Sketch
- death scale
- immediate labor and care loss
- mourning interruption
- succession or appointment conflict
- public legitimacy effects
- long-memory residue

### Imperial Legitimacy Sketch
- throne condition
- succession clarity
- ritual and symbolic moves
- county-level transmission
- obedience versus hollow compliance
- opportunities for court capture or local free play

### Imperial-Local Bargaining Sketch
- central demand
- local intermediaries
- buffering or distortion points
- public compliance versus hidden compliance
- upward appeal channels
- resentment or capture outcomes

### Official Entanglement Sketch
- office position
- household and kin pressures
- clerk and gift channels
- moral cover versus private motive
- public trace left behind
- likely fracture or exposure point

### Office Conflict Sketch
- conflict arena
- rival actors
- procedural weapons
- clerk or docket role
- public effect on governance
- likely freeze or rupture point

### Public Life Sketch
- seasonal events
- gathering spaces
- public attention mechanics
- reputation channels
- crowd triggers
- visible consequences

### Warfare-Lite Sketch
- board objects
- player directives
- state transitions
- aftermath outputs
- anti-patterns to avoid

### Command Culture Sketch
- command temperament
- intelligence posture
- deception opportunities
- terrain logic
- morale handling
- pursuit or restraint rules

### Dynasty Arc Sketch
